name: Multiple myeloma
description: Multiple myeloma — malignancy of plasma cells; one of the APACHE IV chronic health conditions.

en.name: Multiple myeloma
en.shortDescription: Multiple myeloma — malignancy of plasma cells; one of the APACHE IV chronic health conditions.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Multiple myeloma is a malignancy of plasma cells (a type of white blood cell) in the bone marrow, associated with bone disease, renal impairment, anaemia and immunosuppression. In intensive care it is a comorbidity affecting infection risk, renal function and prognosis. Myeloma is one of the chronic health items in the APACHE IV severity model (grouped with leukemia).

## Scope

The set is anchored on the SNOMED concept *437233 / 109989006 — Multiple myeloma* with all descendants included, capturing multiple myeloma and its variants.

## Mapping Notes

- Leukemia and lymphoma are distinct haematologic malignancies captured by their own sets.
